what_it_does: A CSS stylesheet for the surface application that defines the chrome, three responsive layouts (desktop grid, portrait column, landscape rail), and the styling for wheel visualization, disclosure panels, decision/tool modals, settings, scrubber, live indicator, gallery overlay, and an embedded form engine, using CSS custom properties for colors, spacing, and radii.
observations: Uses a token-only approach: colors, spacing, radii, shadows, and typography are referenced via var() and no literal hex colors appear.
Defines three authored layouts through .layout, .layout--portrait, and .layout--landscape classes rather than relying solely on media queries.
Includes a wheel region with portrait aspect-ratio constraints and a tether overlay that draws a dotted SVG line from a selected dot to its detail card.
Implements a disclosure component with three host variants: panel, bottom sheet, and rail.
Provides a verify-mode banner at z-index 9000 that is pointer-events none and only appears for ?verify URLs.
Contains an inbox decisions UI with an entry pill and a modal list, plus a tools face with its own modal and op-conditional form engine (.tf-*).
Implements a gallery overlay modal for drilled-in unit faces, with host-level overrides for DNA .ar-responsive cards, isolation:isolate to contain internal z-indexes, and scrollable content.
Form-factor allocation is driven by body[data-ff] with CSS custom-property fallbacks for resolved allocation values.
Uses color-mix() to create translucent backgrounds and shadows from design tokens.
Includes reduced-motion media query support for the live pulse animation.
Contains extensive inline comments that cite mandates, design critics, and cross-component seams.
standouts: Token-only mandate and three explicit authored layouts.
Gallery-frame host overrides forcing DNA's .ar-responsive card into a single-column, scrollable, phone-width modal and using isolation:isolate to prevent z-index leakage.
Tether overlay: a dotted SVG leader linking a selected wheel dot to its detail card with pointer-events none.
Form-factor allocation via body[data-ff] with resolved-allocation CSS variable fallbacks.
Verify banner at z-index 9000 as a loud but non-blocking dry-run signal.
conventions: BEM-like naming with block--modifier and block-element classes.
Heavy reliance on CSS custom properties for visual values.
Reusable modal overlay pattern shared by inbox, tools, and gallery.
Pointer-events:none for decorative overlays that must not intercept input.
concerns: The file is very large and covers many responsibilities: layout, chrome, modals, form engine, and third-party host overrides.
Despite the token-only color/spacing mandate, several literal pixel sizes remain (e.g., 264px, 320px, 56px, 220px, 390px).
Deep descendant selectors override external library classes (.gallery-frame .screen.ar.ar-responsive), creating tight coupling.
High z-index values (up to 9000) suggest a layering arms race.
Many comments reference transitional/future work and flagged seams, indicating the file is in an intermediate state.
notes: This is the central CSS for the surface visualization app, encoding three responsive layouts and a broad set of chrome, modal, and overlay components, plus host-level overrides for embedding DNA decision cards.
questions: What exact tokens are defined in paper.css and what is the full token vocabulary?
Who sets body[data-ff] and what are the precise classification rules for portrait/landscape/desktop?
What are DNA and fork, and how do their class names (.ar-responsive, .screen, .sbar) relate to this host?
What do the mandate codes (L5/L7/L8, SYNTHESIS §G) and design-critic references refer to?
How is the tether overlay opacity updated, and where does Tether.tsx live?
Are the inbox decisions and tools face independent features or part of the same workflow?
purpose_vs_actual: Broadly matches the stated purpose of surface chrome and three layouts, but the file's actual scope extends well beyond that into modal systems, a form engine, and gallery-host overrides.
apparent_themes: Calm, paper-like UI with subtle ink and pigment accents
Fail-loud but non-alarming notices and verification signals
Modal as a focused single-surface idiom
Host containment and safe embedding of third-party renders
intention_signals: The file is trying to build a polished, responsive visualization surface that remains visually calm across desktop, portrait, and landscape phones, while safely composing external decision/tool renders inside focused modals without letting their viewport-based styles break the host.
novelty: The gallery-frame host bridge is distinctive: it forces an external responsive card archetype into a phone-width modal, makes the card scrollable rather than fixed-height, and uses isolation:isolate to prevent internal z-index leakage over the modal dismiss control.
contribution: A token-based styling layer for surface chrome and three responsive layouts; reusable modal, disclosure, and overlay patterns; a gallery host bridge for embedding external card renders safely; and visual affordances such as the tether selection indicator and wheel-region constraints.
summary_for_embedding: CSS stylesheet for a data-visualization surface application defining token-based layouts, chrome, controls, disclosure panels, decision and tool modals, and host overrides for an embedded DNA card gallery.
intention_for_embedding: To provide a calm, responsive, token-driven visual shell for a wheel-based surface while safely composing external decision and tool renders inside focused modals.
